tasks.task: No code coverage measurement or enforcement — no JaCoCo or equivalent plugin configured, no coverage thresholds. -- Add JaCoCo Maven plugin with coverage reporting and set minimum coverage thresholds (e.g., 70% line coverage for helper/utility code in src/main/java).

CI pipeline (Jenkinsfile) does not execute tests — it only builds and runs SonarQube analysis. Tests are not validated on every push. -- Add a test execution stage to the Jenkins pipeline that runs at least a smoke/regression suite on every build (e.g., mvn test -DsuiteXmlFile=testsuite/Regression.xml).

SonarQube static analysis is disabled (sonar.skip=true in pom.xml line 38), negating the quality gate in the Jenkinsfile. -- Set sonar.skip to false and configure meaningful SonarQube quality gates for code smells, bugs, and security hotspots.

Tests have no isolation from external services — they depend on live QA environments, Redis clusters, and databases. No mocking framework is systematically used. -- For utility/helper unit tests, introduce mocking (Mockito/WireMock) so at least the helper layer can be tested without live infrastructure. Keep integration tests as-is but clearly separate unit from integration tests.

Zero static analysis or linting enforcement — no checkstyle, SpotBugs, PMD, ErrorProne, or formatter is configured or enforced in build or CI -- Add maven-checkstyle-plugin with google_checks.xml or sun_checks.xml to pom.xml and configure it to fail the build on violations. Add SpotBugs or ErrorProne for bug detection. Integrate into the CI pipeline as a blocking step.

No code formatting enforcement — no .editorconfig, no formatter plugin (google-java-format, spotless), no pre-commit formatting hooks -- Add spotless-maven-plugin or fmt-maven-plugin with google-java-format to enforce consistent formatting. Add .editorconfig for basic editor settings.

200+ files use wildcard imports violating project's own stated coding standards, with no tooling to prevent it -- Configure checkstyle AvoidStarImport rule and enforce it in CI. Clean up existing wildcard imports using IDE auto-import organization.

CheckoutJsTests.java is 15,201 lines — a monolithic test class that is extremely difficult to maintain -- Split into focused test classes by feature area (e.g., CheckoutJsPaymentTests, CheckoutJsSubscriptionTests, CheckoutJsEMITests). Each class should be under 1000 lines.

No dependency vulnerability scanning configured. Severely outdated dependencies with known CVEs: jackson-core 2.9.1, jsoup 1.8.3, jjwt 0.9.1, guice 4.1.0, jackson-jaxrs-json-provider 2.7.0. -- Add OWASP dependency-check Maven plugin to pom.xml and integrate into CI pipeline. Update all dependencies to latest secure versions. Consider adding Dependabot or Renovate for automated dependency updates.

Artifact repository URLs in pom.xml use HTTP instead of HTTPS (e.g., http://artifactory-pg-internal.paytm.com), making them vulnerable to MITM attacks. -- Switch all repository URLs to HTTPS to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks during dependency resolution.

Internal infrastructure IP addresses exposed in committed properties files and pom.xml (10.170.x.x, 10.142.x.x, 10.144.x.x ranges). -- Use hostnames instead of IP addresses and move connection details to environment-specific configuration that is not committed to source control.

No post-build actions configured — no failure notifications (email, Slack), no test report archiving, no cleanup steps -- Add a `post { failure { } always { } }` block to the Jenkinsfile for notifications and artifact archiving

System-scoped JARs (checksum.jar, EncryptDecrypt.jar) bundled in src/main/resources/jars/ — fragile, non-reproducible, and breaks Maven dependency resolution. -- Publish these artifacts to the internal Nexus/Artifactory repository and reference them as normal Maven dependencies.

Pervasive use of System.out.println (1593 occurrences) and e.printStackTrace() instead of a logging framework. No logging configuration file exists (no logback.xml, log4j.xml). -- Adopt SLF4J + Logback with a logback.xml config. Replace System.out.println with logger.info/debug and e.printStackTrace() with logger.error(msg, e). This enables log levels, filtering, and structured output for CI.

ReportPortal integration is configured but disabled by default (rp.enable=false). This means centralized test result tracking and historical trends are not available out of the box. -- Enable ReportPortal for CI runs by setting rp.enable=true in the Jenkins pipeline or Maven profile, so test execution history and analytics are automatically captured.

No README.md — a new developer has no primary entry point to understand what this repo is, how to set it up, or how to run tests. -- Create a README.md covering: project purpose, prerequisites, setup instructions, how to run tests, repo layout overview, and links to CONTRIBUTING.md and other docs. Much of this content already exists in CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md and can be consolidated.

No architecture or design documentation — no ADRs, diagrams, or high-level overview of how tests, API helpers, DTOs, and environments relate to each other. -- Add a docs/architecture.md with a component diagram (mermaid) showing test flow: suite XML → test class → API helper → Rest-Assured → PGP endpoints, plus how profiles/environments work.

Extremely sparse inline documentation — most API helper classes and test helpers have no Javadoc, no method-level comments, and no class-level descriptions. -- Add class-level Javadoc to core helpers (PGPHelpers, NativeHelpers, CommonHelpers) and API classes explaining what endpoint each wraps and what parameters mean.

No API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger specs) despite the project being an API test suite covering dozens of PGP endpoints. -- Consider documenting the tested API surface — even a simple markdown table mapping API helper classes to endpoints and their purpose would help onboarding.

5 internal dependencies use -SNAPSHOT versions, making builds non-reproducible — any build can produce different results depending on when it runs. -- Pin internal dependencies to release versions. Use a release process for internal libraries (autumn, utils, pgp-ff4j-strategy, common, cache) and reference stable release versions.

No dependency update automation configured (no Renovate, Dependabot, or similar). Dependencies appear to be manually managed with no regular update cadence. -- Configure Renovate or Dependabot (if using Bitbucket, consider renovate-bot or a scheduled CI job running `mvn versions:display-dependency-updates`).

No <dependencyManagement> section and no maven-enforcer-plugin to ensure dependency convergence and consistent versions across transitive dependencies. -- Add a <dependencyManagement> section or use maven-enforcer-plugin with dependencyConvergence rule to prevent version conflicts.

No release automation or versioning workflow exists -- VERSION file and CHANGELOG were scaffolded by foundry init but are not maintained or integrated into any CI/release process -- Implement a release workflow: use maven-release-plugin or foundry release to automate version bumps, changelog generation, and git tagging on merge to master

Hardcoded credentials committed to git across 36+ config files (DB passwords, JWT secrets, SonarQube token in Jenkinsfile line 19, server passwords in envServerCred.yaml) -- Move all secrets to a vault (e.g., HashiCorp Vault) or environment variables. Use git-secrets or similar pre-commit hook to prevent future credential commits. Rotate all exposed credentials immediately.

No commit message conventions or enforcement — messages are inconsistent (bare JIRA IDs, auto-generated text, vague descriptions) -- Adopt Conventional Commits format and enforce via commitlint or a Bitbucket server-side hook. Require descriptive messages with JIRA ticket references.

.gitignore missing common JVM patterns: *.class, build/, out/, *.log, *.jar, *.pem, *.key -- Expand .gitignore with comprehensive JVM patterns using a template like gitignore.io for Java/Maven/IntelliJ.

No pre-commit hooks or local automation to catch issues before push (no pre-commit-config.yaml, husky, or lefthook) -- Add pre-commit hooks for at minimum: secrets scanning (git-secrets/detect-secrets), commit message format, and basic linting.

No Maven wrapper (mvnw) or JDK version pinning (.sdkmanrc, .java-version). Maven version and JDK version are not enforced, creating 'works on my machine' problems. -- Add Maven wrapper (`mvn wrapper:wrapper`) and a .sdkmanrc or .java-version file pinning JDK 11.

No local development environment setup. Tests require MySQL, Redis, Kafka, MongoDB, Aerospike, and Zookeeper but there is no docker-compose or setup script to provision these locally. -- Add a docker-compose.yml for local service dependencies, or at minimum document clearly that tests require VPN access to internal QA environments.

No Makefile or task runner wrapper. Build/test commands are verbose Maven invocations requiring knowledge of profiles and suite XML files. -- Add a Makefile with `make build`, `make test SUITE=<name>`, `make lint` targets wrapping the Maven commands.

Remove hardcoded credentials from Java source files (HealthCheckHelper.java, RiskVerificationPage.java, OrderStatusAPI.java) -- Read Redis password, risk password, and merchant key from configuration/environment variables instead of hardcoding in Java source. N48 covers config files and Jenkinsfile but not Java source code.

Add release and artifact publishing stages to the Jenkinsfile -- Add a Jenkinsfile stage that publishes the test artifact to internal Maven repository on tagged commits or master merges. N44 covers the release workflow broadly; this is the specific CI pipeline implementation.

Document network requirements for internal artifact repositories and provide offline dependency resolution strategy -- Document that builds require Paytm network access for internal Artifactory/Nexus. Consider providing a dependency cache or offline resolution strategy for external contributors. N38 covers SNAPSHOT pinning; this covers the network access gap.
infra-has-dockerfile -- Add a Dockerfile for containerized deployment
testing-has-conftest -- Add a shared test configuration file (conftest or equivalent)

Add Maven dependency lock file mechanism for fully reproducible dependency resolution -- Add a Maven lock file plugin (e.g., maven-lockfile) to capture and verify the exact resolved dependency tree including transitives. This complements SNAPSHOT pinning (task 20) and dependency convergence (task 22) with a concrete lock file artifact.
security-bandit-in-ci -- Add a static security analysis tool (SAST) to the CI pipeline
security-no-hardcoded-secrets -- Move hardcoded secrets to environment variables

Add general linting (e.g., Checkstyle full ruleset, SpotBugs, PMD) to CI pipeline beyond the wildcard-import check already covered by task 7 -- Configure additional Checkstyle rules, SpotBugs, or PMD in the Maven build and add a dedicated lint stage in the Jenkinsfile, complementing the AvoidStarImport rule from task 7

No coverage enforcement: JaCoCo is configured for reporting only (prepare-agent + report goals) but has no 'check' goal and no minimum coverage thresholds. -- Add a JaCoCo check execution with minimum line/branch coverage ratios (e.g., 60% for helper/API classes) to fail builds when coverage drops.

No branch-specific pipeline triggers -- the Jenkinsfile has no `triggers`, `when` blocks, or PR-specific stages -- Add `when { branch 'master' }` conditions or `pipelines.pull-requests` equivalent to differentiate PR checks from master builds
Dockerfile runs as root — no USER directive present in the runtime stage -- Add a non-root user: RUN useradd -r appuser && USER appuser after COPY steps in the runtime stage.

Base Docker image eclipse-temurin:11-jdk is not pinned to a specific patch version (e.g., 11.0.22_7-jdk-jammy) and uses full JDK instead of JRE for runtime -- Pin to a specific image digest or patch version tag, and use eclipse-temurin:11-jre for the runtime stage to reduce attack surface.
Some internal Artifactory URLs in settings.xml use HTTP instead of HTTPS, allowing potential MITM of dependency downloads -- Switch all repository URLs to HTTPS.

System.out.println used 1,438 times across 201 files instead of SLF4J logging — test output is unstructured, unleveled, and impossible to filter or aggregate -- Migrate all System.out.println calls to SLF4J logger. Start with listeners (StatusCounterListener, CustomListener, ExecutionListener) and helper classes, then test classes. Use log.info for lifecycle events, log.debug for detailed steps.

No structured logging (JSON) — logback.xml uses plain text pattern format only, making log aggregation and searching difficult -- Add logstash-logback-encoder or configure JsonLayout in logback.xml for machine-parseable output, especially useful for CI/CD log aggregation.

OWASP dependency-check plugin is configured but not included in the CI pipeline (Jenkinsfile). Vulnerability scanning is only available manually. -- Add a dedicated 'Dependency Check' stage to the Jenkinsfile that runs `dependency-check:check` and fails the build on critical CVEs.
No .gitattributes file for line ending normalization or LFS tracking -- Add .gitattributes with '* text=auto' for line ending normalization and configure Git LFS for binary files (jars, images)

The `make lint` target is a no-op placeholder that just echoes 'ok' — no actual local linting runs -- Wire `make lint` to run `spotless:check` and/or `checkstyle:check` locally so developers get fast feedback before CI.

Split PGPHelpers.java (1,924 lines) into domain-specific helper classes -- Extract PGPHelpers methods into focused helpers by domain (e.g., RefundHelpers, EMIHelpers, UPIHelpers). Keep PGPHelpers as a thin facade if needed for backward compatibility.

Replace e.printStackTrace() calls with log.error('message', e) across all files -- Find all e.printStackTrace() usages in PassedReport, LogsValidationHelper, PGPBaseTest, and other files and replace with log.error() calls that include exception context.

Populate CHANGELOG.md with historical release notes from git history -- Backfill the changelog with historical entries from git log. Use git-cliff or manual curation to create meaningful release note sections for past versions.

Purge committed secrets from git history and rotate all exposed credentials -- Use BFG Repo-Cleaner or git filter-repo to remove secrets from git history. Rotate all exposed credentials (DB passwords, JWT keys, Jenkins deployer password, Redis passwords). Add localconfig.properties and envServerCred.yaml to .gitignore.

No metrics or instrumentation of any kind — no test execution duration metrics, API response time tracking, or failure rate monitoring beyond basic TestNG listener counters. -- Integrate Micrometer or a simple StatsD/Prometheus client to emit test execution metrics (pass/fail rates, API latencies) that can be consumed by monitoring dashboards.

CustomExtentITestListenerAdapter (enhanced error classification for test failures) is entirely commented out, removing what would have been useful failure diagnostics. -- Either restore and update the CustomExtentITestListenerAdapter with proper exception classification, or implement equivalent diagnostics in the active FailureHandlingListener.

HealthCheckHelper exists for Kafka, Zookeeper, and Redis but is only called conditionally (INITIATE_LOGCHECK flag) and not systematically before every test run as a precondition check. -- Integrate HealthCheckHelper into the BeforeSuite/ExecutionListener flow to fail fast with clear error messages when infrastructure is unavailable.

Inline code documentation is inconsistent. Test classes largely lack class/method-level Javadocs, making it harder for new contributors to understand test intent without reading full implementations. -- Add class-level Javadocs to test classes describing what feature area they cover, and method-level comments for complex test flows. Prioritize the most-used helper classes.

Split ProcessTransactionTests.java (~12K lines) and PGOnly.java (~8K lines) into focused test classes by feature area -- Apply the same splitting strategy used for CheckoutJsTests (task 8) to ProcessTransactionTests and PGOnly. Enable checkstyle FileLength enforcement (max=3000) to prevent future growth.

Document the deployment model and add IaC configuration for the test suite beyond local docker-compose -- Document how the test suite is deployed and orchestrated in CI/QA environments. If it runs in Kubernetes, add minimal manifests or Helm chart. If deployment lives in another repo, document the reference.
Enable static analysis on test sources -- Set includeTestSourceRoots=true in checkstyle, includeTests=true in SpotBugs, add test source directories to checkstyle sourceDirectories.
Add build failure email notifications to Jenkinsfile -- Configure emailext in post.failure block so team is alerted when CI breaks.
Add MDC context for environment/profile/suite in ExecutionListener for structured log correlation.
Synchronise VERSION file and pom.xml parent version to 3.3.0.
Add rollback documentation (docs/rollback.md) covering git tag rollback, Jenkins rollback, and post-rollback checklist.
Add docker-compose.yml for local dev infrastructure (MySQL, Redis, MongoDB, Kafka, Zookeeper).
Fix make release to be non-interactive (VERSION=x.y.z parameter) and add setup/infra-up/infra-down targets.
Add Bitbucket PR template (.bitbucket/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md) for standardised PR descriptions.
Update CONTRIBUTING.md with current lint tools (Spotless, Checkstyle, SpotBugs), Makefile commands, and branch naming conventions.
Add troubleshooting guide (docs/troubleshooting.md) covering build failures, test failures, CI issues, environment setup.
Remove hardcoded credentials from settings.xml -- Use env var references (${env.MAVEN_REPO_USER}, ${env.MAVEN_REPO_PASSWORD}).
Add dependencyManagement section and dependencyConvergence enforcer rule for consistent dependency versions.
Replace SQL string concatenation with SafeQueryBuilder across PGPHelpers, SavedCardHelpers, ReqAuthUPICreateOrder.
Add class-level Javadoc to helper classes (CorporateHelpers, IrctcHelper, LoyalityPointsHelper, EncryptedCardData, PWPHelpers, RefundStatusV1Helper).
Add unit test infrastructure with SafeQueryBuilderTest and testsuite/unit-tests.xml.
Add Jenkinsfile Release stage for automated git tagging on master.
Update Maven plugins: compiler 3.12.1, surefire 3.2.5, resources 3.3.1.

Some tests have assertion bugs — e.g., MerchantNotifyTest.java:41-43 calls String.contains() without asserting the result, meaning those checks silently pass even on failure. -- Replace bare String.contains() calls with assertThat(...).contains() or Assert.assertTrue(str.contains(...)) throughout the test suite.

Hardcoded test data (merchant IDs, encrypted card data, request IDs) scattered across test files reduces maintainability and environment portability. -- Extract test data into constants classes, data providers, or configuration files tied to the test profile.

Checkstyle is configured but non-blocking: failsOnError=false and failOnViolation=false in pom.xml, meaning lint violations never fail the build -- Set failOnViolation=true in the maven-checkstyle-plugin configuration. Start by enforcing error-severity rules only, then gradually tighten

SpotBugs static analysis is advisory-only (failOnError=false), so security and bug findings from FindSecBugs never block merges -- Set failOnError=true for SpotBugs. Use spotbugs-exclude.xml to suppress known false positives rather than disabling enforcement entirely

Binary public.key file tracked in src/main/resources/addMoneyExpress/public.key — while public keys are lower risk, tracking key material in git is a bad practice -- Consider loading key material from a secrets manager or external config, and add key files to .gitignore

Trivy security scan configured with exit-code: 0, meaning it will never fail the build regardless of vulnerabilities found -- Set exit-code to 1 (at least for CRITICAL severity) so that critical vulnerabilities block the pipeline

No distributed tracing or correlation IDs propagated through API calls to backend services under test -- Add OpenTelemetry or a correlation ID header (X-Request-Id) to REST Assured requests so test traffic can be traced in backend observability tools

No error reporting integration (Sentry, Rollbar) for capturing and alerting on test infrastructure failures -- Integrate Sentry or similar service for alerting on unexpected test infrastructure errors (not test assertion failures)

Maven lacks a native lock file mechanism, so dependency resolution can vary across environments. The settings.xml points to internal Artifactory/Nexus repos which may not be accessible to all agents. -- Consider using maven-dependency-plugin to generate a dependency tree snapshot, or adopt a Maven wrapper version lock. Document internal repo access requirements clearly for agents.

Set a unit test coverage target (20-30% of test files) and add unit tests beyond the mocking/isolation scope of task 4 -- After mocking infrastructure is in place (task 4), systematically add unit tests for business logic classes like SafeQueryBuilder pattern applied to other helpers, tracking coverage percentage.

Split CashierPage.java (4,153 LOC) into sub-components using Page Object pattern -- Break CashierPage.java into smaller page object classes by UI section/feature area. Tasks 8 and 58 already cover the test class splits (CheckoutJs, ProcessTransaction, PGOnly).

Create the initial semantic version tag (v3.3.0) on master to bootstrap the release workflow -- Manually tag the current master as v3.3.0 to establish the versioning baseline that the release automation (task 23) will build upon.

Enforce commitlint via CI pipeline in addition to local pre-commit hooks -- Add a CI pipeline step that runs commitlint on PR commits to prevent bypassing local hooks with --no-verify. Task 25 covers adopting conventions and local enforcement.
Add 5 new unit test classes (EncryptedCardDataTest, PGPHelpersUtilTest, LocalConfigTest, TraceIdGeneratorTest, ValidatePassCodeTest) covering helper utilities, config resolution, and security checks.
Add CorrelationIdFilter REST-assured filter injecting X-Request-Id header and MDC context. Registered globally in PGPBaseTest.
Parallelise Lint and Security Scan stages in Jenkinsfile. Run SonarQube on all branches.
Add historical released versions (3.2.0, 3.1.0) to CHANGELOG.md.
Add envServerCred.yaml and localconfig.properties to .gitignore to prevent accidental credential commits.
Bind Spotless to Maven verify phase for automatic formatting enforcement.
Fix silent no-op .contains() assertions in MerchantNotifyTest (17 occurrences wrapped in Assert.assertTrue).
Add Javadoc to PGPBaseTest, CreateUpiLinkApi, GetPaymentStatus, TxnStatusApi, FastForward.

SQL injection risk in test helpers: Multiple files (OTPInjection.java, PG2MappingApisHelper.java, etc.) build SQL queries via string concatenation instead of using the SafeQueryBuilder utility -- Refactor all SQL query construction in test helpers to use SafeQueryBuilder.builder().sql(...).quoted(...).build() pattern as documented in SafeQueryBuilder.java

Parent POM (parent-automation:3.3.0-SNAPSHOT) may override child dependencyManagement declarations, causing version resolution issues (e.g., jackson-databind resolving to 2.9.0.pr1 despite child override) -- Run 'mvn dependency:tree -Dverbose' to identify conflicts. Either fix parent POM or use explicit exclusions in child POM. Consider flattening the POM hierarchy if parent provides little value.

No evidence of rollback automation or deployment verification - rollback is manual via git checkout of tags -- Add automated rollback scripts or integrate with deployment orchestration tools. Consider smoke test automation post-release to enable automatic rollback on failure

Add Docker image push to container registry in CI pipeline -- Implement docker push step in Jenkinsfile to publish built Docker images to internal container registry, separate from Maven artifact publishing covered in task 32

Identify and pin the 6th SNAPSHOT dependency not covered in task 20 -- Task 20 mentions 5 SNAPSHOT dependencies. Audit pom.xml to identify the 6th SNAPSHOT (parent-automation:3.3.0-SNAPSHOT) and include it in the pinning effort

Troubleshoot and verify Renovate bot is operational after configuration -- Task 21 covers configuring Renovate. Add verification step: check Bitbucket settings, review Renovate logs, ensure bot credentials are configured, and validate first automated PR is created

Add automated changelog generation to release workflow -- Task 23 covers release workflow broadly. Specifically integrate git-cliff or conventional-changelog into the release pipeline to automate CHANGELOG.md generation from conventional commits
